where we might want to translate a grayscale image to color, a noisy image to a clean one, a blurry image to a sharp one, or a sketch to a photo-realistic image.

This section discusses three image translation models that use different amounts of manual labeling. The Pix2Pix model uses before/after pairs for training. Models with adversarial losses use before/after pairs for the main model but also exploit unpaired “aft

The Pix2Pix model (figure 15.16) is a network $\hat{\mathbf{x}} = \mathbf{g}[\mathbf{c}, \boldsymbol{\theta}]$ that maps one image $\mathbf{c}$ to a different style image $\mathbf{x}$ using an adversarial discriminator $f[\mathbf{c}, \mathbf{x}, \phi]$, which
